title: After class
summary: A young teacher in her first year is told her class is boring. She finds the janitor in an empty classroom, and he tells her what he has heard for twenty-eight years.

body.speaker: Trang
body.text: Bác Sang, can I ask you something? I have been a teacher for one year. Today I had a hard day.

body.speaker: Bác Sang
body.text: Sit. I am cleaning anyway.

body.speaker: Trang
body.text: A student told me my class is boring. Right to my face.

body.speaker: Bác Sang
body.text: What grade?

body.speaker: Trang
body.text: Year nine. A boy. One of my better students. That is why it hurt.

body.speaker: Bác Sang
body.text: Is your class boring?

body.speaker: Trang
body.text: I do not know. I work hard. I prepare.

body.speaker: Bác Sang
body.text: Working hard does not make a class interesting.

body.speaker: Trang
body.text: What does?

body.speaker: Bác Sang
body.text: I have been here twenty-eight years. I clean after class. I hear what students say when the teacher leaves. I know which classes they remember.

body.speaker: Trang
body.text: Which ones?

body.speaker: Bác Sang
body.text: The ones where the teacher made a mistake.

body.speaker: Trang
body.text: A mistake?

body.speaker: Bác Sang
body.text: Said the wrong year for a war. Forgot a name. And then handled it well — laughed, or thanked the student who corrected them. Those classes the students talk about for weeks.

body.speaker: Trang
body.text: But I am supposed to know everything.

body.speaker: Bác Sang
body.text: Says who?

body.speaker: Trang
body.text: The school. The parents. Me, I guess.

body.speaker: Bác Sang
body.text: Trang, you are twenty-five. You cannot know everything. The students know you cannot. When you pretend you can, they get bored. When you tell the truth, they wake up.

body.speaker: Trang
body.text: I have never thought of it that way.

body.speaker: Bác Sang
body.text: Most teachers do not. I have watched more teachers than any teacher has.

body.speaker: Trang
body.text: Why are you telling me this?

body.speaker: Bác Sang
body.text: I have a granddaughter in year nine.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for unfinished time: 'I have been…'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'have/has been' với khoảng thời gian để nói về một trạng thái bắt đầu trong quá khứ và còn tiếp diễn. Trong bài: 'I have been a teacher for one year.' (Tôi đã làm giáo viên được một năm.) / 'I have been here twenty-eight years.' (Tôi đã ở đây hai mươi tám năm.)
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'have/has been' with a period of time for a state that started in the past and continues. From the dialogue: 'I have been a teacher for one year.' / 'I have been here twenty-eight years.'

blanks.explanation: Trang has been a teacher for one year.
A student told her the class is boring.
Year nine — and Bác Sang has a granddaughter in the same year.
It hurt because the student was one of her better ones.
Bác Sang has been at the school twenty-eight years.
He knows which classes the students remember.
The memorable classes are the ones where the teacher made a mistake and handled it well.
Said the wrong year for a war — a memorable kind of mistake.
Trang is twenty-five — too young to know everything, and that is fine.
When you tell the truth, they wake up — they become attentive.
Bác Sang's reason: a granddaughter in year nine.

translationVi:
Trang: Bác Sang, cháu hỏi bác một chuyện được không? Cháu đi dạy được một năm. Hôm nay cháu có một ngày khó.
Bác Sang: Ngồi đi. Bác cũng đang lau lớp.
Trang: Có một học sinh nói thẳng vào mặt cháu rằng giờ học của cháu chán.
Bác Sang: Lớp nào?
Trang: Lớp chín. Một cậu bé. Một trong những học sinh khá của cháu. Vì vậy cháu đau lòng.
Bác Sang: Vậy lớp của cháu có chán không?
Trang: Cháu không biết. Cháu làm việc chăm. Cháu chuẩn bị.
Bác Sang: Chăm không làm cho một giờ học hay.
Trang: Vậy cái gì làm cho hay?
Bác Sang: Bác ở đây hai mươi tám năm. Bác lau lớp sau giờ học. Bác nghe các em nói gì khi cô thầy rời lớp. Bác biết lớp nào các em nhớ.
Trang: Lớp nào ạ?
Bác Sang: Những lớp mà thầy cô mắc lỗi.
Trang: Mắc lỗi?
Bác Sang: Nói nhầm năm của một cuộc chiến. Quên một cái tên. Rồi xử lý lỗi đó tốt — cười, hoặc cảm ơn học sinh chữa lại. Những lớp đó các em nói chuyện về cả tuần.
Trang: Nhưng cháu phải biết mọi thứ chứ?
Bác Sang: Ai nói vậy?
Trang: Nhà trường. Phụ huynh. Cả chính cháu, chắc vậy.
Bác Sang: Trang, cháu hai mươi lăm. Cháu không thể biết mọi thứ. Học sinh biết là cháu không thể. Khi cháu giả vờ là biết, các em chán. Khi cháu nói thật, các em tỉnh ra.
Trang: Cháu chưa bao giờ nghĩ theo cách đó.
Bác Sang: Hầu hết thầy cô không nghĩ vậy. Bác đã quan sát nhiều thầy cô hơn bất kỳ thầy cô nào.
Trang: Sao bác kể cho cháu điều này?
Bác Sang: Bác có một cháu gái học lớp chín.

questions.prompt: How long has Trang been a teacher?
questions.explanation: 'I have been a teacher for one year.'

questions.prompt: What did a student tell her?
options: Her class is boring — right to her face
questions.explanation: 'A student told me my class is boring.'

questions.prompt: Why did it hurt her especially?
options: The boy is one of her better students
She is new at the school
questions.explanation: 'One of my better students. That is why it hurt.'
questions.hint: Look at Trang's reply about the grade.

questions.prompt: How long has Bác Sang been at the school?
questions.hint: Look at Bác Sang's long turn.

questions.prompt: What does Bác Sang say makes a class memorable to students?
options: The teacher made a mistake — and handled it well, laughing or thanking the student who corrected them
questions.explanation: 'The ones where the teacher made a mistake.' And then handled it well.
questions.hint: Look at Bác Sang's turn about mistakes.

questions.prompt: Who does Trang say expects her to know everything?
options: The school, the parents, and herself
questions.explanation: 'The school. The parents. Me, I guess.'
questions.hint: Look at Trang's reply to 'Says who?'

questions.prompt: What does Bác Sang say happens when a teacher pretends to know everything?
questions.explanation: 'When you pretend you can, they get bored.'
questions.hint: Look at Bác Sang's longer turn about pretending.

questions.prompt: And when a teacher tells the truth (about not knowing)?
questions.explanation: 'When you tell the truth, they wake up.'

questions.prompt: Why is Bác Sang telling Trang all this?
options: He has a granddaughter in year nine and would like her teachers to know what makes a class worth remembering
He used to be a teacher
questions.explanation: 'I have a granddaughter in year nine.'
questions.hint: Look at the very last turn.

questions.prompt: What is the tone of the conversation?
options: Warm and pointed — the older janitor gives the young teacher honest, practical advice
questions.explanation: He treats her as a peer who can hear hard advice, and ends with the personal reason behind it.
questions.hint: Notice how he sets the bar and then explains why he cares.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Bác Sang says: 'When you pretend you can, they get bored. When you tell the truth, they wake up.' Think of a role you play where you feel pressure to know everything. What would change for the people around you if you admitted what you do not know?
